skill.name: technical-writing
skill.description: Write clear, comprehensive technical documentation. Use when creating specs, architecture docs, runbooks, or API documentation. Handles technical specifications, system design docs, operational guides, and developer documentation with industry best practices.
skill.summary: Write clear, comprehensive technical documentation. Use when creating specs, architecture docs, runb...
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 82 static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation templates with markdown code examples, example URLs in API documentation, and placeholder environment variable names. No actual code execution, network requests, or credential access occurs. This is a benign technical writing assistance skill.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Formatting Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 53 locations. These are markdown inline code examples (e.g., `curl`, `kubectl logs`) used in documentation templates, not actual shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks are markdown inline code formatting in documentation templates, not actual Ruby/shell execution. No exec(), spawn(), or system() calls present.

low_findings.title: Example URLs in Documentation Templates
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Hardcoded URL' at 4 locations. These are example placeholder URLs (e.g., api.example.com) in API documentation templates, not actual network connections.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs like 'https://api.example.com/endpoint' are placeholder examples in documentation templates. No actual fetch, curl, or HTTP client calls exist.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Environment Variable in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Generic API/secret keys' at line 435. This is a placeholder example ('API_KEY: Your API key') in a configuration documentation template.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable shown is a documentation example, not actual secret access. No process.env, os.environ, or getenv() calls present.

content.value_statement: Produce clear, comprehensive technical documentation including specifications, architecture docs, runbooks, and API docs with industry best practices.
actual_capabilities: Write technical specifications with goals, non-goals, and implementation plans
Create architecture documentation with diagrams and component descriptions
Build operational runbooks with deployment steps and troubleshooting guides
Generate API documentation with authentication, endpoints, and examples
Produce changelogs and release notes in standard formats
Structure documentation for different audiences (developers, ops, stakeholders)
limitations: Does not execute code or verify technical accuracy of documented solutions
Does not generate actual diagrams (only provides Mermaid syntax templates)
Cannot access external systems or APIs to verify documentation claims
Requires user to provide accurate technical details about their system

use_cases.title: Create Technical Specifications
use_cases.description: Generate detailed technical specs for new features including problem statements, solution design, and implementation plans.

use_cases.title: Document System Architecture
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive architecture documents with component descriptions, data flows, and technology stack details.

use_cases.title: Write Operations Runbooks
use_cases.description: Build operational guides for deploying, monitoring, and troubleshooting services.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Technical Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a technical specification for a [feature name] that includes: overview, problem statement, goals and non-goals, solution design, implementation plan, and testing strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a system architecture document for [system name] that includes: high-level overview, components with responsibilities, data flow, technology stack, and security considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Operations Runbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an operations runbook for [service name] covering: service overview, deployment steps, rollback procedures, monitoring metrics, common issues, and troubleshooting commands.

prompt_templates.title: API Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate API documentation for [API name] including: authentication requirements, endpoint definitions with parameters, request/response examples, and error codes.

output_examples.input: Write a technical specification for a user authentication feature
output_examples.output: A structured document with sections: Overview, Problem Statement, Goals (secure login, token management), Non-Goals (social login, password recovery), Solution Design (data models, API contracts), Implementation Phases, Testing Strategy, Security Considerations

output_examples.input: Create an API documentation template for a payments API
output_examples.output: Documentation with: Authentication section (Bearer token), endpoints (POST /payments, GET /payments/:id), request/response examples in JSON, error codes table, and integration guidelines

best_practices: Match writing style and technical depth to your target audience
Include code examples that readers can test and verify
Keep documentation synchronized with code through regular reviews
anti_patterns: Assuming reader knowledge without defining terms
Using passive voice instead of active voice
Including outdated information without noting the version

faq.question: What document types can this skill create?
faq.answer: Technical specifications, architecture documents, runbooks, API documentation, changelogs, and user guides.

faq.question: Does this skill verify that my code works?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates documentation templates and content. You must verify technical accuracy yourself.

faq.question: Can it generate actual diagrams?
faq.answer: It provides Mermaid diagram syntax that you can render in compatible markdown viewers.

faq.question: How do I customize for different audiences?
faq.answer: Use the audience-specific guidelines in Step 1 of the skill. Adjust technical depth, focus areas, and terminology accordingly.

faq.question: Can Claude Code execute the commands in the documentation?
faq.answer: No, the skill only provides documentation templates. Any commands shown are examples for documentation purposes.

faq.question: How often should I update technical documentation?
faq.answer: Update documentation when making significant code changes, at minimum during each release cycle.
